description: §65.2.4 R1 — a universal `*` rule is an author-reset FLOOR (a LOWER layer, §65.5), not a same-level conflict. `* { padding: 0 }` overlapping `.btn { padding: 16px }` on `<button class="btn">` is a deterministic layer (the author expects the specific rule to win), NOT E-STYLE-CONFLICT. The commonest opening lines of a stylesheet must not false-fire — neither the hard error nor the soft warning.
rationale: §65.2.4 R1 (ratified 2026-07-08): universal `*` / bare-root (`html`/`body`) rules resolve BELOW class/id/specific author rules in the §65.5 precedence order — an overlap between them and a specific rule is a deterministic LAYER, not a conflict. This carve-out removed 65% of would-be-hard findings in the §65.11 dry-run with zero loss of real signal.
